input.background: Patients receiving chemotherapy as outpatients were randomized to receive either an active antiemetic treatment or placebo (Williams et al., 1989). The following table shows measurements (in mm) on a 100 mm linear analogue self-assessment scale for nausea.
input.question: Identify and carry out an appropriate analysis to compare the values in the two groups.

output.answer: The linear analogue scale data do not meet the distributional assumptions for parametric methods based on the $t$ distribution. The groups can be compared using the non-parametric Mann-Whitney test, which gives $z=3.29, mathrm{P}=0.001$. There is thus strong evidence that nausea was less severe in patients receiving the active treatment.
